div1.entryFree: Shoeing-horn, a horn used to facilitate the entrance of the foot into a narrow shoe; emblem of one who is a subservient tool to the caprices of another: a thrifty s. in a chain, hanging at his brother's leg, Troil. V, 1, 61 (alluding, at the same time, to the cuckoldom of Menelaus).
